understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each level by placing all selectable pieces onto the goal tiles marked '+' at the same time; if there were multiple goals, every goal needed to be occupied simultaneously for the level to register WIN, and completing all levels would win the full game run within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each action moved the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction unless blocked by walls/objects or constrained by level boundaries; some adjacent objects could potentially be pushed if directly in the movement direction, while walls acted as immovable blockers (with 'movement.*_blocked' only being a hint, not a guarantee). When multiple selectables existed, control could be switched using CLICK on the exact pixel/grid coordinates of another selectable as listed in the state, instantly changing which piece subsequent MOVE actions affected.
